description: Update project documentation to reflect the network init change: update docs/technical.md and any relevant spec files to document that loopback initialization now uses u-root's netlink-based approach and requires kernel 6.8+.
steps: Read docs/technical.md and docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md to understand current documentation of the rc/loopback functionality
Update docs/technical.md to document that loopback initialization uses u-root libinit.NetInit() via netlink, and note the kernel 6.8+ requirement
If docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md references loopback setup, update it to reflect the new approach
Update docs/status.md if it tracks migration progress to note the network init migration is complete
Run golangci-lint and make lint to ensure no issues

Commit with message: 'docs: update documentation for netlink-based loopback init

Document that loopback initialization now uses u-root libinit.NetInit()
which leverages the netlink API. Note the kernel 6.8+ requirement where
the kernel automatically configures 127.0.0.1/8 when lo is brought up.'
acceptance_criteria: Documentation references u-root libinit.NetInit() for loopback initialization
No stale references to /sbin/ip for loopback setup in docs
findings: docs/technical.md updated with Network Init row in the Technology Stack table (u-root/pkg/libinit, vishvananda/netlink, kernel 6.8+). docs/status.md updated with completed feature entry. docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md had no loopback-specific content to update - it only mentions 'k3os rc' at a high level. docs/PRD.md mentions 'configures loopback networking' but this remains accurate as a functional description regardless of implementation.
